body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: provider ping failure classifier
body.intention: I intend that any unintended effects of adding _classify_ping_failure() to the Source base class are observed and documented.
body.description:
**What changed:** ProviderHealthClassifier added; Source base class gains _classify_ping_failure() called in all subclass ping() except blocks.
**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Silent perf regression: classify() is called on every ping failure; if ProviderHealthClassifier is slow or raises, it blocks the except branch and delays fallthrough.
- Log noise: INFO lines fire on every ping failure (Ollama is pinged frequently); could flood inference logs if a source is persistently unreachable.
- Missing subclass coverage: if a Source subclass has a bare `except:` that doesn't call _classify_ping_failure(), failures go unclassified silently.
- Exception masking: if _classify_ping_failure() itself raises, it could shadow the real exception in logs.
**Signals to watch:** inference log volume before/after; any new exception tracebacks in datacenter_logs/inference/; whether all subclass ping() except blocks actually call _classify_ping_failure().
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-26 or after first DS run following deploy.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
